description: Validate friction-controlled rolling motion on a flat support surface. The smoke family uses a ball with initial horizontal velocity, but the invariant applies to any rolling rigid body with contact friction.
physical_assumptions: The rolling body starts with a declared horizontal velocity.
The rolling body remains in contact with a support surface.
Dynamic or rolling friction should reduce speed over time.
Travel distance should stay inside the expected friction response envelope.
verifier_rules: Horizontal speed decays by the expected amount.
Forward displacement is within the friction-bounded distance range.
Rolling body does not reverse direction without an external impulse.
repair_suggestions: Declare initial horizontal velocity and support surface contact.
Bind friction material metadata to the rolling body and surface.
Tune friction or solver substeps only after trajectory/contact evidence exists.
Use an external force capability if motion accelerates or reverses.
